observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Local Development Council (LDC) to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan the required resolution to facilitate the re-alignment/ re-programming of PPAs; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the disbursement vouchers and other transaction documents on a timely manner; and (ii) strengthen review function on the requisite documentation of procurements to support the claims; (c) the Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Technical Working Group to ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurement processes through public bidding and alternative modes of procurement; and (d) Bids and Awards Committee to avoid splitting of contracts and employ appropriate mode of procurement for all government transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
LDC did not submit to the Sangguniang Bayan the required resolution to facilitate the re-alignment/ re-programming of PPAs for CY 2023. There were delayed submission of transaction documents that still occurred in CY 2024. Lapses in the procurement process still occurred during CY 2024. Splitting of contracts still occurred in CY 2024.
observations.reason: The Local Development Council did not submit the required resolution to the Sangguniang Bayan; delayed submission of transaction documents occurred; lapses in procurement and splitting of contracts occurred in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the OIC-
Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to continue
adhering with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 96-011 to ensure reliability and accuracy of presentation in the
financial statements of the Cash-in-Bank Local Currency-Current
Account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Accountant to (i) continue to set-up RPT/SET Receivables
based on Certified List of Taxpayers provided by Municipal Treasurer’s
Office; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the prior
years’ set up of RPT/SET Receivables merely based on estimates; and (b)
OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly update the RPTAR to properly
identify the delinquent tax payers; (ii) introduce other collection strategy
that would significantly help increase the revenue from RPT/SET
Receivables of the Municipality; (iii) institute applicable remedies,
administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes
as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and
(iv) undertake the necessary measures to fully utilize the main features of
the Real Property Tax System to enhance the operations and revenue
collection.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) exert best effort to trace
from old accounting records the details and ownership of Cash
Advances/Due from Officers and Employees totaling ₱1,728,187.78 which
lack supporting records; (ii) initiate the procedures for the application of
request for write-off of dormant accounts prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2023-008; and (iii) institute appropriate remedies that would require
the identified Accountable Officer to settle or liquidate his past due
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Treasurer to consider increasing the amount of Fidelity Bond
of SDOs whose cash advances exceeded the maximum cash
accountabilities; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that only
bonded Accountable Officers are allowed to receive cash advances; (c) all
officials and employees to discontinue the practice of using cash advances
for procuring goods and services unless the nature of transactions are
deemed very difficult, impractical or impossible to be paid by check; and
(d) BAC to (i) strictly adhere with the requirement that suppliers must be
legally registered businesses before transacting to them; and (ii)
conduct thorough verification, including checking of Business Permits,
Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) Registration, and PhilGEPS
Membership.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Accountant to (i) properly follow the steps of recording
incoming and out-going inventories in a perpetual method of inventory
keeping; and (ii) strengthen review function on the requisite
documentation of procurements to support the claims for payment; (b)
OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to do the reconciliation of all
inventory accounts to determine the correct balances; (c) OIC-GSO to (i)
maintain complete stock cards of Inventories; (ii) consolidate weekly the
RIS in the SSMI and submit the same to the Office of the Accountant; (iii)
undertake complete physical count of all inventories of the Municipality;
and (iv) prepare the corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the
Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (d) requesting officials
and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentary
requirements as noted in audit to support the payment to suppliers of
welfare goods for distribution; and (e) provide sufficient workforce to
GSO to address manpower deficiency, if warranted.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to present complete
details in the RPCPPE when conducting physical count; (b) OIC-General
Services Officer to (i) maintain complete property records; and (ii)
reconcile all the PPE records with the Accounting records to establish
correctness of the balances of PPE accounts and settle discrepancies
identified during the process; (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the
Infrastructure assets that were previously transferred to the Registry of
Public Infrastructure to their appropriate PPE accounts; and (d)
Property Disposal Officer to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE
in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.
Furthermore, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and
OIC-General Services Officer consider the guidelines on one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to make proper
coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure
projects to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the
appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the
financial statements; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) cause thorough
review of accounting and related records to determine the composition of
the forwarded balances of the CIP accounts to establish the particular
completed projects which remained recognized under the CIP accounts
and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments, if warranted; (ii) record
the balance of the unpaid billing on the completed project as Accounts
Payable to complete the recorded cost of the CIP and reclassify the cost of
the completed projects from CIP account to the appropriate PPE account;
(iii) record the depreciation expense of the completed project from the
time of completion; and (iv) compute and impose liquidated damages on
the contractor for failure to complete the projects within the contract
time; and (c) Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation
of the awarded projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
implementing offices to continue adhering with the guidelines and
requirements on the efficient utilization of funds received from the
National Government Agencies for the implementation of various
programs, projects, and activities; and (b) OIC - Municipal Accountant to
continue monitoring the status of funds received by maintaining
individual subsidiary ledger accounts and properly return all unexpended
balances to the respective Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) MHRMO
to continue updating and reconciling the leave credits of employees of the
Municipality; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant, OIC-Municipal
Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to take coordinated actions
to increase revenue collections and allocate sufficient budgetary
provisions to facilitate the recognition of expense and liability accounts for
employee benefits.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Treasurer to regularly conduct examination of the books of
accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel who are
equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination
of the books of accounts to determine the truthfulness of the declared
gross sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid and to
collect the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of the RA No. 7160;
(b) BPLO to fully enforce the submission of Sworn Statement of Gross
Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax Return from taxpayers to
verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as provided by Section
2E.04 (d) and (e) of the Revenue Code; and (c) OIC-Municipal
Treasurer/BPLO to cause recovery of the under collection of ₱57,007.48
arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the sampled business
establishments.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) OIC- MPDO to (i)
avail the technical assistance of the Department of Human Settlements and
Urban Development (DHSUD) in updating its Comprehensive Land Use
Plan (CLUP) to come up with a more effective and specific development
goals; (ii) ensure the correlation of the plans between CLUP, CDP, Local
Development Investment Program (LDIP) and AIP for a more focused
implementation process; (iii) meticulously prepare the Annual Investment
Program and only include developmental projects that could practically or
feasibly be carried out within the budget period; and (iv) conduct a cost-
benefit analysis to assess whether the conversion of the facility into an
events place will generate higher income compared to its previous use; (b)
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, and Technical
Working Group to ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA
No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements through public bidding and
alternative modes of procurement; (c) Municipal Engineer to (i) strictly
monitor the implementation of the awarded projects to ensure that timely
completion of infrastructure projects as scheduled; and (ii) ) submit to the
OIC-Municipal Accountant the necessary documents related to the demolished
part of the structures in Cabiao Floating Market including the list of scrap
materials to effect the necessary dropping from the books of the cost of the
said structure; (d) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) strengthen review
functions on the required documentations of transaction to support the
claims for payment before signing box A of Disbursement Vouchers; and
(ii) compute and impose liquidated damages on the contractor for failure
to complete the projects within the contract time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal HRMO to (i) ensure that the hiring of JO workers is based on
specific projects or programs supported by a detailed plan including the
objectives, deliverables and duration; (ii) implement control measures on
submission of DTRs and Accomplishment Reports of JO workers; and (iii)
validate the correctness of information in the DTRs and Accomplishment
Reports submitted by JOs before forwarding the same to the Accounting
Office; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of
supporting documents to substantiate the claims.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive require the (a)
OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the adjustment of error made in
recording the transfer of unspent LDRRMF; (ii) ensure that the rules and
regulations in the transfer of unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF are
properly and strictly followed; (iii) submit the lacking documentary
requirements on the procurements of equipment and various supplies and
materials totaling ₱6,893,174.67; (iv) ensure that supporting documents
were properly and completely filled-up; and (v) strengthen her review
function on the requisite documentation of all transactions; and (b)
Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Officer (i) stop the
practice of drawing cash advances for the procurement of equipment and
various supplies and materials; and (ii) coordinate with the OIC-
Municipal Budget Officer regarding update/revision of AIP for the
projects and activities to be implemented and submit the required report
to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the fund utilization to avoid
spending beyond the approved budget for each PPA; (b) Local School
Board to prepare the necessary request for realignment/reprogram of
funds and submit to Municipal Budget Office to properly align the SEF
expenditures to the approved appropriations; (c) BAC to (i) ensure
compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised
IRR in all procurement processes through public bidding and alternative
modes of procurement; and (ii) avoid the splitting of contracts and employ
the appropriate mode of procurement for the transactions; (d) Municipal
Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of the awarded projects
to ensure that these are completed as scheduled; and (e) OIC-Municipal
Accountant to compute and impose liquidated damages on the contractor
for failure to complete the projects within the contract time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GFPS to (i)
furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report
vis-a-vis the GAD Plan and Budget to facilitate the review process; (ii)
create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis of preparing the annual GAD
Plan and Budget; (iii) carefully plan the corresponding budget
allocations of GAD PPAs by preparing project breakdown of expenses
before the approval of the plan and budget; and (iv) conduct a review
after three years and an end-term evaluation to reflect on the status of
accomplishment, variances, and overall assessment of the
implementation of the GAD agenda based on the goals and desired
results/outcomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief executive require the (a) BAC to (i)
ensure that the GCC and SCC are included in the bidding documents;
(ii) affix their initials or signatures in all the pages of the bidding
documents submitted by the bidders; and (iii) require the bidders to
submit the approved CSHP by DOLE; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant in
coordination with the Municipal Engineer to (i) submit the “As-Built”
plans of completed projects, reviewed and certified by the Municipal
Engineer’s Office; and (ii) ensure that the required documents for
infrastructure projects are complete before payment.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the MENRO to (i) review and update the Solid Waste
Management Plan every two years or as the need arises, to ensure the
sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and
international developments in the field of solid waste management; (ii)
intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on the
regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay
officials; (iii) rigorously implement the Municipal Ordinances in relation
to SWM and impose sanctions to violators, if warranted; and (iv)
accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection”
and “no littering” policies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) admonish
their duties and functions on the submission of financial transactions and
related reports as sanctioned by COA; (ii) submit immediately all
unsubmitted transactions and reports for CY 2024; and (iii) henceforth,
observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission
particularly on the submission of financial transactions which is within
the first 10 days of the ensuing month; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant
to conduct proper review and ensure completeness of supporting
documents before signing box A of Disbursement Voucher.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a)
Department Heads to constantly monitor the fund balances of their
respective offices and comply strictly with their Project Procurement
Management Plans (PPMP); (b) OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to refrain
from accommodating prior year’s expenditures to be charged against the
current year appropriations unless these are covered by unexpended
balances from prior appropriations that have been reverted back to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund at year-end and are duly
supported by an appropriate legislative enactment; and (c) OIC-
Municipal Accountant to ensure that only lawful expenditures and
obligations incurred during a fiscal year shall be recognized or taken up
in the accounts of that year. Furthermore, we recommended that the
Local Chief Executive to (i) investigate and determine the accountability
of concerned personnel for the irregular obligations and disbursements of
funds, and implement appropriate administrative sanctions, if warranted;
and (ii) require the Municipal Department Heads, OIC-Municipal Budget
Officer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit justification on lapses
of charging prior year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to (i) establish specific
and clear policies and guidelines on the grant of medical assistance for
dialysis patients, such as the qualification of beneficiaries, amount and
frequency of assistance, documentary requirements needed, and the like,
concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, pursuant to Section 2 of PD No.
1445 to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds; and
(ii) revisit the terms of the MOA entered with Cabiao Dialysis Center, in
coordination with the legal and financial experts to ensure that the
provisions are aligned with public interest and do not result in undue
disadvantage to the Municipality; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
to (i) explain/justify why the default mode of procurement which is public
bidding was not observed in the procurement of medical services; and (ii)
observe the rules and regulations on procurement process and procedures
to ensure that the most advantageous prices will be obtained for the
government; and (c) OIC-Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review
function on the requisite documentations of various transactions to
support the claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a)
Requesting Officials to conduct thorough planning and forecasting before
requesting the quantities of meals/snacks in Purchase Requests to ensure
alignment with the anticipated number of attendees as means to
implement control, such as requiring accurate headcounts or registration
confirmation before meal procurement to avoid incurring excessive
expenditures; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) strengthen the review
function on the requisite documentation of procurements to support the
claims before signing on box A of the DVs; and (ii) submit the DVs and
other transaction documents on a timely manner particularly for the
transaction documents amounting to ₱1,673,804.80 pertaining to meals
and/or snacks; and (c) Requesting Officials and OIC-Municipal
Accountant to submit the lacking documentary requirements as noted in
audit to support the payment to suppliers of meals and snacks.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate full compliance
on the publicity and notification of the Municipality’s PPAs by: (a)
notifying the Audit Team within ten days after the award of infrastructure
projects that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are
already posted in the project sites; and (b) instructing the Municipal
Engineer to (i) closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins by
the contractor/supplier on the project site, and recommend the immediate
rectification had there been incomplete details or erroneous inscription;
and (ii) ensure that all required project details are properly filled out and
regularly updated throughout the construction period until completion,
allowing constituents and stakeholders to exercise their right to
information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue the
faithful compliance with the pertinent rules and regulations on the
withholding of taxes, mandatory contributions and loan repayments from
employees/contractors and the timely remittances thereof to the
concerned government agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to exercise due prudence in
the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit
suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue to adhere with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ensure reliability of presentation in the financial statements of the Cash-in-Bank, Local Currency-Current Account; and (ii) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each PPAs under the Trust Fund to monitor the cash balances at any given period.

observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (i) continue to adhere
with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996 to ensure reliability of presentation in the financial
statements of the Cash-in-Bank, Local Currency-Current Account; and (ii)
maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each PPAs under the Trust Fund to
monitor the cash balances at any given period.

2.12 We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require
the (a) Accountable Officers to improve their compliance on the liquidation
or settlement of the cash advances granted to them; (b) Municipal
Accountant to intensify collection measures or institute appropriate
remedy that would require Accountable Officers to liquidate/settle their
past due accounts; and (c) if the conditions or circumstances on writing off
the accounts are met, direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the
procedures for the application of the request for write-off of dormant
accounts, under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

3.28 We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive instruct
the (a) Inventory Committee to (i) present the complete details of PPE items
in the RPCPPE when conducting physical count; and (ii) facilitate the
disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines
and procedures; (b) ICO-Municipal Treasurer/GSO to (i) maintain
complete property records; and (ii) reconcile all PPE accounts with the
Municipal Accountant to establish correctness of the PPE balances; and (c)
Municipal Accountant to record the Infrastructure Assets that were
previously transferred to the Registry of Public Infrastructure to their
appropriate PPE accounts. Furthermore, we recommended that the
Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer consider the
guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE
information.

4.17 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant and OIC-Municipal Engineer to make proper coordination on
timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects to
subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate
asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements;
(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the balance of the unpaid billing on
the completed project as Accounts Payable to complete the recorded cost of
the CIP and reclassify the cost of the said completed project from CIP
account to the appropriate PPE account; (ii) record the depreciation
expense of the completed project from the time of completion; and (iii)
impose the computed liquidated damages on the contractor for failure to
complete the projects within the contract time; and (c) OIC-Municipal
Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of the awarded projects to
ensure that these are completed as scheduled.

5.19 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
implementing offices to continue adhering with the guidelines and
requirements on the efficient utilization of funds received from the National
Government Agencies for the implementation of various programs,
projects, and activities; and (b) Municipal Accountant to continue
monitoring the status of funds received by maintaining individual
subsidiary ledger accounts and properly return all unexpended balances to
the respective source agencies.

7.25 We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the
optimal utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund (LDRRMF) through a more realistic project planning and budgeting;
and (b) exert more effort to achieve full adherence to rules and regulation
provided in R.A. 10121 or the Philippine Risk Reduction and Management
Act of 2010 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1 on
the revision and reporting of 5% LDRRMF.

8.20 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
MENRO to (i) review and update the Solid Waste Management Plan every
two years or as the need arises, to ensure the sustainability, viability,
effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international
developments in the field of solid waste management; (ii) intensify the
information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid
waste management in coordination with barangay officials; (iii) rigorously
implement the Municipal Ordinances in relation to SWM and impose
sanctions to violators, if warranted; and (iv) accelerate efforts in the
enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” and “no littering” policies.

9.20 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Business
Permit and Licensing Officer (BPLO) to (i) regularly review and monitor
the correct application of the Municipal Revenue Code; (ii) verify that the
designated rates and amounts are correctly applied according to the
taxpayer's business type in accordance to Municipal Revenue Code; (iii)
mandate business taxpayers to provide a sworn statement of gross
sales/receipts for the initial tax computation and furnish a copy of their
Certified ITR for more adequate computation of the deficiency in tax
calculation, if any; and (iv) enhance the strictness application,
implementation and enforcement the Municipal Revenue Code to all the
businesses; and (b) BPLO, ICO-Municipal Treasurer and Sangguniang
Bayan to (i) consistently review and revise the Municipal Revenue Code
every five years, as mandated by law, to maximize revenue generation for
the welfare of constituents; and (ii) guarantee that any amendments to the
Revenue Code correctly reflect the prevailing economic circumstances and

of information and supporting documents before signing on box A of
Disbursement Vouchers.

Local Finance Committee to make a more systematic and realistic
forecasting to set-up attainable performance targets on all possible sources
of income and to continue with campaign efforts for maximum collection
efficiency.

13.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue the
faithful compliance with the pertinent rules and regulations on the
withholding of taxes, mandatory contributions and loan repayments from
employees/contractors and the timely remittances thereof to the concerned
government agencies.

disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer ensure that all unreleased checks at the end of the year are reported and properly treated in accordance with the Manual on NGAS.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the (a) ICO-Municipal Treasurer, to maintain the integrity of RPT/SET receivable accounts by maintaining reliable records and imposing stricter controls on updating the receivable information/data; (b) accountable officers, to improve further their compliance on the reporting/liquidation of cash advances; (c) Municipal Accountant, to intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that would require accountable officers to settle or liquidate their past due accounts; and (d) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts would be present, direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the procedures for the application of the request under COA Circular 2016-005.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) the Inventory Team to present complete details in Report on Physical Count on PPE when conducting physical count; (b) the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete PPE records such as PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards for the proper control and monitoring of the agency property and equipment; and (c)  the Municipal Accountant to record the Infrastructure assets that were previously transferred to the Registry of Public Infrastructure to their appropriate PPE accounts. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) institute remedial measures to cure the defects of unauthorized expenditures under the 20% Development Fund and henceforth, prudently observe the legal parameters and comply with rules and regulations on the use of said fund; and (b) instruct Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) strictly adhere with the procurement process and procedures as prescribed under Revised IRR of R.A. 9184; and (ii) submit the copies of the lacking documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the LDRRM Officer to cause the submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor, OCD and DILG-LGOO as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local School Board and Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all SEF disbursements are supported with complete documentation and the execution of PPAs are in accordance with the guidelines and parameters prescribed by DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017; and (ii) facilitate the immediate processing of deed of donation so that the construction of classrooms project could push through.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in his capacity as Chairman of the GFPS to (i) issue Executive Order to complete the composition of the GFPS including its ExeCOM and TWG relevant for gender mainstreaming; (ii) instruct the GFPS to create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making; (iii) employ the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (iv) henceforth, inform the DILG in writing for any changes in the GPB before the implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) convene all responsible heads of offices and admonish them of their duties and functions on the submission of the accounts, financial records and reports as sanctioned by COA; and (ii) instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately submit all transactions documents including the disbursement vouchers, and their supporting documents; and (b) henceforth, observe the timeliness of reportorial requirements of the Commission.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to put in place the sound internal controls over cash transactions by (i) monitoring the activities of the collectors and reviewing thoroughly their reports to avoid the lapses above mentioned; (ii) diligently verifying their accountabilities over collections and forms and conducting the physical count and inspection activities; (iii) avoiding the delegation of collection functions to non-permanent employees; and (iv) conducting proper investigation for the occurrence of fraudulent transactions, determining all persons responsible and liable on the irregularities and filing appropriate charges, if necessary, as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) maximize the use of electronic payroll system in payment of salaries and other benefits of the employees to minimize the risk, if done through cash advance; and (b) monitor the maximum accountability allowed for the existing Fidelity Bond, if warranted, request for an increase thereof to an amount sufficient to cover the maximum accountability for a given period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Engineer to (a) include the cost of tarpaulin/signboards in Program of Works, (b) closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins detailing the complete and relevant information of Programs Projects Activities (PPAs) implemented by the Municipality, and (c) document the compliance of the contractor for the said work item.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Local Finance Committee, to timely prepare the required reports and observe prudence on the disclosure policy prescribed by the guidelines to promote accountability and transparency to the public; (b) the OIC-Municipal Engineer, to strictly monitor implementation of awarded projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled; and (c) impose liquidated damages on the contractor for failure to complete the projects within the specified contract time.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality are insured against any insurable risk with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) to properly protect the government properties in the event of serious loss such as fire, earthquake, typhoon and/or flood. Henceforth, procure only the insurance of properties from the GSIS GIF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated March 31, 2018.

We commended the management for its continuous efforts to comply with the COVID-19 vaccination program guidelines, a way of providing protection to their constituents from adverse effects of the virus.  We also recommended that the management continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity or 70% of the target population can be attained in the soonest time possible.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant (a) to prepare complete and accurate remittance reports of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from suppliers/contractors/employees to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth; (b) exert extra effort to settle the negative prior year’s balance of Due to GSIS or facilitate the reconciliation to bring the account balances accurate; and (c) update regularly the posting of transactions in the Subsidiary Ledgers to ensure correctness of the reported periodic balances.
We recommended and management agreed to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical count of all property, plant and equipment and the inventories of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE and Inventories and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards; (c) designate General Services Officer who shall maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies and PPE, respectively; and (d) require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE and Inventories accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and furnish copy to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers, and maintain subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.

We reiterated our previous recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) stop the practice of granting new cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; (b) require the concerned personnel to liquidate cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished; (c) avail of the administrative and/or legal remedies, if necessary in case of continued failure of accountable officer to liquidate the outstanding cash advances; and (d) monitor timely submission of liquidation reports and completeness of supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) identify the Road Network previously transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure and recognize the same as part of the Infrastructure Asset account; and (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Treasurer (Acting General Services Officer) in the preparation of the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) and inclusion in the disclosure of the total road networks in the Notes to the Financial Statements; (b) Municipal Treasurer-(Acting General Services Officer) to (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office in the conduct of inventory of local road network yearly; (ii)  prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System, Report on Local Road Network, and (iii) maintain the Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and (c) Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer (Acting General Services Officer) and Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements of COA Circular 2015-008 dated February 23, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Sanggunian to (a) strictly conform to the guidelines set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and (b) refrain from charging expenditures that are not aligned in the fund’s intended purpose.

We recommended that the Local School Board headed by the Local Chief Executive and District Supervisor as co-Chairman to (a) conduct a thorough review and evaluation on the planning and budgeting practices of the Municipality to ensure that all expenses incurred are among the allowable transactions that should be charged to the fund in compliance with DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) conduct proper review of Disbursement Vouchers before certifying completeness of documents to ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation; and (ii) cause the submission of the lacking documents for review and validation of the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and ensure that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRMM programs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Acting MENRO to (i) continue to embark effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s Ecological Solid Waste Management Program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment; (b) take appropriate actions to expedite the construction of Central Material Recovery Facility (MRF) of the Municipality; and (c) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining five Barangays in establishing their own MRF.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Market Supervisor to (i) ensure that all lessees have valid contracts; (ii) limit the contract period to two years; and (iii) secure authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with the lessee; and (iv) initiate extra efforts to enforce collection of unpaid rentals and impose penalties on delayed payments, as the case maybe; (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to monitor payment of rental of each stallholders; and (c) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Official Receipts are properly and completely accomplished to include stall numbers and rental period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Human Resources and Management Officer to review and verify the submitted DTRs and forwards the same to the Accounting Office for preparation of payrolls; and (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the payrolls based on the submitted Daily Time Records (DTRs) to avoid overpayment; and (ii) ensure that claims are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) all Department Heads to (i) judiciously plan each activities and make an accurate count of the participants attending to limit the quantity of purchased food items; and (ii) exercise prudence in utilizing funds which are intended for public purpose; and (b) Municipal Accountant ensure that Disbursement Vouchers are supported with complete documentation to support the claim before signing the box A of Disbursement Voucher.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the General Services Officer to (i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to (a) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality to the GSIS; and (b) ensure that all insurable assets and properties are insured against any insurable risk with the GIF of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law, as amended.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to adopt a system of payroll payment through Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of the Government Banks in order to protect government funds and minimize operating costs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) strictly comply with the provisions set forth in Section 2.Q of COA Circular No. 92-389 by regularly stamping the “PAID” marking to disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents upon payment to prevent possible re-use of the same, and avoid misappropriation of government funds; and (b) indicate the check number and actual date of payment.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure that the GAD Focal Point System are in place and fully functional; (b) strictly comply with the provisions of the Magna Carta of Women and PCW-DILG –DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to adhere to the revenue regulations on the withholding and prompt remittance of taxes to the BIR.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere to the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG; and (b) Municipal Accountant to allot time to investigate, analyze and reconcile records pertaining to incurrence of abnormal balances of Due to Pag-IBIG transferred to Other Payable account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth; and (b) Municipal Accountant to allot time to investigate, analyze and reconcile records pertaining to incurrence of abnormal balances of Due to PhilHealth transferred to Other Payable account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the copies of the PMR to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality observed the intact withholding and remittance of GSIS contributions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 

(a) ensure that in the preparation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the Municipality for CY 2017, the required composition of the Council be fully represented; and 

(b) strictly considered in compliance with Section 2 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (PDRRM) Act of 2010.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality issued Executive Order No. 3 series of 2017 to comply with the required composition of the LDRRMC per Section 2 of the IRR of RA No. 10121.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the affected accounts involved by debiting the Cash in Bank to reflect its actual balances and crediting the Accounts Payable to restore the liability to concerned creditors in the amount of ₱7,736.02.  

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer is advise to send written notices to payees of checks which has long been outstanding a month before said checks become stale.  

Strict adherence to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks should be observed by concerned officials of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The adjustedment for stale checks were already effected in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/ disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management ensure that prudence is observe in disbursement of government funds.
observations.reason: The disallowance of ₱66,000.00 is not yet settled.

observations.managementAction: Management have already instructed the concerned officials to comply with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all agency officials concerned revisit and observe the abovementioned provisions of Section 325 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the preparation of the succeeding annual budget of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Same condition still exists as the previous years.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our earlier recommendation that Management instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection and Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to the Office of the Auditor to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

Considering that the subject deficiencies had been repeatedly discussed in prior years’ report,   there is a compelling reason that the recommended courses of actions and corrective measures offered should be given serious consideration by the Management.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the concerned officials to comply with the recommenda-tions governing submission of contracts to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Compliance to the recommenda- tions was not given proper attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore reiterated our recommendation that management  instruct the committee to complete the physical inventory taking of its property, in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Gather all vital information for each PPE which are absolutely necessary to facilitate/establish reliable basis for the computation of the corresponding depreciation. 

Reconcile the result of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record.  Follow the procedures listed in Section 491 of GAAM Volume I. For facility, we recommend that all offices start the count at their respective offices and the General Services Office to consolidate the result. Maintain property ledger card for each PPE item.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has gave instructions to concerned officials to complete the actual physical inventory of PPE.
observations.reason: The absence of reliable PPE inventory report affected the reliability of the reciprocal report on depreciation expenses.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) effect the necessary adjusting entries for expense items totaling
₱232,898.92 which were included in the PPE account balance; (ii) reconcile and verify
based on the available records such as Check Disbursement Journals/Report of
27
Check Issued the nature of transactions of PPE amounting ₱1,712,215.19 which have
no complete details of information; and (iii) provide an allowance for impairment
losses for PPE in compliance with PPSAS No. 17; and (b) ICO-Municipal Treasurer
regularly conduct the annual inventory count for all property, plant and equipment
at year-end and submit the RPCPPE as required by COA Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant investigate, analyze and
reconcile records pertaining to incurrence of abnormal balances of Due to Pag-IBIG
and Due to GSIS accounts to determine any errors that needs adjustment to reflect
the correct balances of accounts.

3.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (a) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the
liquidation of previous ones; (b) require the concerned personnel to liquidate cash
advances as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished; (c) send demand
letters to require liquidation of the cash advances; (d) monitor timely submission of
liquidation reports and completeness of supporting documents in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (e) avail of the remedial
30
measures provided for in Sections 7.0 and 8.0 regarding the write-off of dormant
receivable accounts from former deceased elected officials.

4.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) take appropriate action to
expedite the construction of the new Material Recovery Facility (MRF) of the
Municipality; (b) ensure that the existing MRF of the seven component Barangays
become fully operational; (c) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining
16 Barangays to establish their own MRF; (d) instruct the Municipal Engineer to
prepare separate collection schedules for different types of wastes to avoid mixed
collection of wastes; and (e) include representatives from the recycling and the
manufacturing or packaging industry to be the members of the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board (MSWMB).

5.4 We recommended and management agreed to observe the limit and comply
with Section 325 of RA No. 7160. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor and OIC-Municipal
Budget Office are advised to ensure that the filling–up of vacant position and
hiring/promotion of personnel shall be suspended until the required 45/55 PS
limitation is achieved.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustments to correct the error committed by the previous Municipal Accountant in
recognizing the unexpended portion of the Priority Development Assistance Fund
(PDAF) amounting to ₱500,000.00 as income for Calendar Year 2012.
6.6 We also advised the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant
and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized portion of the PDAF to the
Bureau of Treasury the soonest time possible.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (i) Municipal
Accountant to (a) prepare the payrolls base on the actual services rendered by
employees as shown on the approved Daily Time Records (DTRs) duly signed by both
the employee and his/her supervisor; (b) sign payrolls if completely supported with
approved DTRs and Application for Leave as the case maybe; and (c) re-compute the
salaries and allowances that should have been paid to employees for the month of
November-December 2016 using the corresponding DTRs of each employee to
determine any discrepancy between the amount paid and should have been paid and
deduct any overpayment from the succeeding payroll or leave credits.; and (ii) OIC-
Municipal Human Resources and Management Office (a) ensure the timely
submission of approved DTRs thru the Electronic Data Processing (EDP) Office
under Mayor’s Office and Application of Leave of employees to the Accounting
Office; and (b) forward to Accounting Office the validated DTRs together with the
approved Application for Leave as the case maybe of all the employees to be used as
basis of the Accounting Office in the preparation of the payroll.

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the (a) Municipal School
Board to identify the needed teachings/instructional materials of the DepEd school
teachers to facilitate the procurement/acquisition thereof; (b) OIC-Municipal Budget
Office ensure that budget items under SEF funds are within the priority
disbursements enumerated in Republic Act No. 5447, Section 272 of Republic Act No.
7160 also known as the Local Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 and No. 01-A dated March 14,
2000; and (c) Municipal Accountant conduct proper review of supporting documents
to ensure the validity and legality of the claims before certifying Box A of the
disbursement vouchers.

9.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: (i) the Members of
the Municipal Local School Board to prepare and cause the approval of the annual
School Board Budget for SEF by prioritizing programs, projects and activities as
stipulated in Section 1 of R.A. No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of R.A. No. 7160,
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, and No. 01-
A, March 14, 2000; and (ii) the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant
to stop the practice of utilizing the fund for payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office
and other expenses not related to or included in the list of prioritized expenses
chargeable against SEF.

10.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (i) Municipal
Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten
(10) days following the end of the month in compliance to Section 6 of Revenue
Regulation No. 10-93 to avoid sanctions as stated in Section 272 of Republic Act No.
8424 otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act; and (ii) Municipal Accountant to (a)
cause the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher for taxes withheld but not yet
remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2016; and (b) trace any unrecorded or
erroneous entry caused by the abnormal balance of Due to BIR account of ₱1,576.24
under Trust Fund account.

11.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer to (a) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and
Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality; and (b) ensure that
all insurable assets and properties be insured against any insurable risk with the GIF
of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property
Insurance Law, as amended.

12.5 We recommended and management agreed to furnish the Audit Team with
the copies of Memorandum Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement of all funds
transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in Item 3.1.2
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to facilitate evaluation and audit of disbursements
from the said fund transfers.

13.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
and all other concerned officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 on the submission of a list of all on-going PPAs
as well as those to be implemented during the year and the respective monthly
monitoring reports to enable the Audit Team to conduct prompt validation of the said
PPAs and timely submission of Quarterly Report to the COA Regional Director.

14.5 We recommended that the GAD Focal Person strictly adhere with the
provision of Section 5.5 of JMC 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA
regarding submission of Accomplishment Report not later than end of January of the
ensuing year.

15.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to follow the provision on the monthly net take home pay of Three
Thousand Five Hundred Pesos (₱3,500.00) for all the regular employees and the order
of preference of the authorized deductions to avoid over deductions of loans and
contributions from salaries and other benefits of the employees concerned as
mandated by Section 52 of Republic Act No. 10717, otherwise known as the General
Appropriation Act for FY 2016.

16.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to stop the practice of charging payment of membership dues of
league/association of appointive officials against funds of the Municipality in
compliance with Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) Opinion
No. 99 dated July 3, 2002.

17.11 We recommended and management agreed to (a) ensure that all trainings,
seminars, workshops or similar activities undertaken/conducted by the Municipality
and participated by its officials, be made within their territorial jurisdictions in
compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012; and (b) require the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer to submit the lacking documents for the conduct of verification of the Audit
Team.

18.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and
submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in
compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

19.5 We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
and ICO-Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory
contributions withheld to the GSIS.

20.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that in the preparation of
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the
Municipality for CY 2017, the required composition of the Council be fully
represented and strictly considered in compliance with Section 2 of the Implementing
Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management (PDRRM) Act of 2010.

21.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the affected accounts
involved by debiting the Cash in Bank to reflect its actual balances and crediting the
Accounts Payable to restore the liability to concerned creditors in the amount of
₱7,736.02. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer is advise to send written notices to
payees of checks which has long been outstanding a month before said checks become
stale. Strict adherence to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of
stale checks should be observed by concerned officials of the municipality.

22.6 We recommended and management agreed to exercise due prudence in the
disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

We therefore reiterated our recommendations that:

The Local Chief Executive 
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of using trust fund for payment of regular expenditures;
cause the immediate remittance of trust liabilities due to other concerned government agencies;
instruct the Budget Officer / Accountant 
control the disbursements based on the available funds/appropriation and not on the available cash balance; and

ensure that trust fund should be made available and use only for the specific purpose for which it was created and/or received to avoid the recurrence of similar lapses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality observed austerity measures in disbursing government funds which resulted in cash available of ₱3.3 million at year-end  and ensure that trust fund will be used only for its intended purpose.

Recoveries of some unliquidated cash advances were effected thru salary deductions. Likewise the Municipal Accountant assigned personnel to monitor the timely submission of Liquidation Reports of officials and employees with cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances with an aggregate amount of ₱1.63 million pertained to the former mayor and vice mayor who are both deceased.

observations.recommendation: For the timely monitoring of accountabilities of the municipal treasurer on various accountable forms and to address the apparent weakness in monitoring of its utilization, the team recommended religious compliance of the aforementioned official with the above regulation.
observations.managementAction: Mothly Reports of Accountabili-ty for Accountable Forms for the months of January to December 2016 were already  submitted by the ICO-Municipal Treasurer’s Office to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) instruct the MSWMB to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval; 

(b) exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Sanitary Landfill; and

 (c) strictly enforce implementation of the ordinances pertaining to solid waste management other relevant policies and intensify information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection” policy and “no-littering” policy.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted a copy of the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan to National Solid Waste Management Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive appropriate at least five percent (5%) of the agency’s total appropriations for Gender and Development (GAD) programs.  Likewise, ensure that all activities included in the mentioned program are GAD-related.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality totaled to ₱10,852,229.72 or 7.10% of the CY 2016 Annual Budget of the Municpality of ₱152,836,668.57.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to continuously perform its function as withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Section 6 of the Revenue Regulation No. 10-93 of the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: The management committed to remit intact the taxes withheld in CY 2017.
observations.reason: There were delayed remittance of taxes for the 1st semester of CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality continue to remit all compulsory contributions withheld promptly to the GSIS.  We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the negative balance of Due to GSIS account and make the necessary adjusting entry in the books, if any.
observations.managementAction: The remittance of premiums and loan repayments were religiously made by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management conduct complete physical inventory of its property in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Reconcile the result of the actual physical inventory report with the accounting record Follow the procedures listed in Section 491 of GAAM Volume I. For facility, we recommend that all offices start the count at their respective offices and the General Services Office to consolidate the result. Maintain property ledger card for each PPE item.
